back_translation: And they told David, saying, 'Behold, the Philistines are fighting against Keilah, and they are plundering the threshing floors.'
And David inquired of the LORD, saying, 'Shall I go and strike against these Philistines?' And the LORD said to David, 'Go and strike against the Philistines, and save Keilah.'
And David's men said to him, 'Behold, we here in Judah are afraid; how much more if we go to Keilah against the ranks of the Philistines?'
And David inquired again of the LORD, and the LORD answered him, and said, 'Arise, go down to Keilah, for I am giving the Philistines into your hand.'
And David and his men went to Keilah and fought against the Philistines, and led away their livestock, and struck them with a great blow, and David saved the inhabitants of Keilah.
And it happened, when Abiathar son of Ahimelech fled to David at Keilah, that an ephod came down in his hand.
And it was told to Saul that David had come to Keilah, and Saul said, 'God has delivered him into my hand, for he has shut himself in by entering a city of gates and bar.'
And Saul summoned all the people to war, to go down to Keilah, to besiege David and his men.
And David knew that Saul was devising evil against him, and he said to Abiathar the priest, 'Bring near the ephod.'
And David said, 'O LORD God of Israel, Your servant has heard indeed that Saul seeks to come to Keilah to destroy the city for my sake.
Will the lords of Keilah deliver me into his hand? Will Saul come down as Your servant has heard? O LORD God of Israel, tell please Your servant.' And the LORD said, 'He will come down.'
And David said, 'Will the lords of Keilah deliver me and my men into the hand of Saul?' And the LORD said, 'They will deliver.'
And David and his men, about six hundred men, arose and went out from Keilah and went wherever they could go. And it was told to Saul that David had escaped from Keilah, and he ceased to go out.
And David stayed in the wilderness in the strongholds and stayed in the hill country in the wilderness of Ziph. And Saul sought him every day, but God did not give him into his hand.
And David saw that Saul had come out to seek his soul, and David was in the wilderness of Ziph at Horesh.
And Jonathan son of Saul arose and went to David at Horesh, and strengthened his hand in God.

And he said to him, 'Do not be afraid, for the hand of Saul my father will not find you, and you will be king over Israel, and I will be to you as second-in-command. And even Saul my father knows this.'
And the two of them made a covenant before the LORD. And David remained at Horesh, and Jonathan went to his house.
And the Ziphites went up to Saul at Gibeah, saying, 'Is not David hiding with us in the strongholds at Horesh, on the hill of Hachilah, which is to the right of Jeshimon?
And now according to all the desire of your soul, O king, to come down — come down — and ours is delivering him into the hand of the king.'
And Saul said, 'Blessed are you of the LORD, because you have had compassion on me.
Go please, prepare further, and know and see his place where his foot will be, and who has seen him there, for I am told that he is extremely cunning.
And see and know from all the hiding places where he hides, and return to me with certainty, and I will go with you. And if he is in the land, I will search for him among all the thousands of Judah.'
And they arose and went to Ziph ahead of Saul, and David and his men were in the wilderness of Maon in the Arabah to the right of Jeshimon.
And Saul and his men went to seek him, and they told David, and he went down to the rock and stayed in the wilderness of Maon. And Saul heard and pursued after David in the wilderness of Maon.

And Saul went on one side of the mountain, and David and his men on the other side of the mountain. And David was hurrying to go away from the face of Saul, and Saul and his men were surrounding David and his men to seize them.
And a messenger came to Saul, saying, 'Hurry and come, for the Philistines have raided against the land.'
And Saul returned from pursuing after David, and went to meet the Philistines. Therefore they called that place Sela-hammachleqot.
